claimText: The Minamata Convention is a legally binding treaty effective from 2017, and India is a member state.
evidenceExcerpt: The Minamata Convention (effective 2017) is a legally binding treaty to protect human health and the environment from mercury; India is a member.
examUse: Statement-based question on the nature of the convention and India's participation.

claimText: COP-6 to the Minamata Convention set a target to phase out mercury-based dental amalgams by 2034.
evidenceExcerpt: COP-6 agreed to phase out mercury-based dental amalgams by 2034 and eliminate mercury-added skin-lightening products.
examUse: Statement-based question on recent targets set under the Minamata Convention.

claimText: Mercury is a naturally occurring heavy metal and the only common metal that is liquid at room temperature.
evidenceExcerpt: Mercury is the only common metal that is liquid at room temperature; anthropogenic sources include coal-based thermal power plants and gold mining.
examUse: Science and technology statement-based question on the physical properties of mercury.

claimText: Coal-based thermal power plants and gold mining are major anthropogenic sources of mercury emissions.
evidenceExcerpt: anthropogenic sources include coal-based thermal power plants and gold mining.
examUse: Multiple-choice question identifying sources of heavy metal pollution.

claimText: Mercury emitted into the environment converts into highly toxic methylmercury, which bioaccumulates in aquatic food chains.
evidenceExcerpt: Mercury converts into highly toxic methylmercury, leading to bioaccumulation in fish and animals.
examUse: Statement-based question on the ecological and chemical behavior of mercury in food webs.
distractorHooks: Does not bioaccumulate in aquatic life

claimText: There is no known safe level of mercury exposure for humans.
evidenceExcerpt: There is no known safe level of mercury exposure for humans.
examUse: Statement-based question on the health impacts and safety thresholds of mercury.
